steps.title: Connect songs to ecology
steps.content: The songs preserved knowledge of terraced clam gardens. Chen and colleagues used archaeological evidence too, finding that the practice described in the songs corresponded with larger and more abundant clams despite greater harvesting pressure.

steps.title: Reject overclaims about evidence
steps.content: Choice A overclaims that songs are the only reliable source. Choice C denies the relationship the finding establishes. Choice D wrongly ranks archaeological evidence above song-based knowledge. The finding shows that practices preserved in Indigenous songs can be valid ecological knowledge for science. The answer is B.
